channel.title: CSRF on Evan Lecklider
channel.description: Recent content in CSRF on Evan Lecklider

item.title: Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention in Sinatra
item.description: Unlike Rails, Sinatra has no native cross-site request forgery prevention so I thought I’d take a minute to outline the implementation that I use to prevent these kinds of attacks. It’s pretty naive and simple, but that’s the kind of implementation I like. If you’re unaware, cross-site request forgery (CSRF) is a very common vulnerability that exploits an existing session in a user’s browser and the inherent trust that establishes with the server to carry out malicious acts.
